title: Will Eva ever be laid to rest
display_title: Will Eva ever be laid to rest
wikitext:
[]: Will Eva ever be laid to rest?             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Sun Jun 01, 2008 12:03 am How much longer is the Eva franchise gonna pump out new shows, movies, and manga? For awhile it seemed it was only gonna continue on in new manga only, but some new movies have come out. Will the franchise ever stop pumping new material, or will we be old geezers still seeing Rebuild of Rebuild of Rebuild of Rebuild of Evangelion?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jun 01, 2008 12:04 am This may fall under http://www.evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=4876

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Sun Jun 01, 2008 12:10 am Not quite what I was thinking, Sachi. I'm just curious if others think there may be even more of a reboot to the franchise than just the Rebuild movies and its tie ins.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sun Jun 01, 2008 4:29 am Providing there's a suitable ending supplied by Rebuild, I don't see why there should be more movies or tv. I'm sure even Anno has limits.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Jun 01, 2008 4:34 am To be honest I was horribly let down when I heard about Rebuild

[]:             Quote Post by Wevangeliwon » Sun Jun 01, 2008 5:50 am master_lloyd wrote: Providing there's a suitable ending supplied by Rebuild, I don't see why there should be more movies or tv. I'm sure even Anno has limits.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sun Jun 01, 2008 6:45 am As long as there is money to be made Gainax will whore it out. Probably even after Anno is burried in the ground.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Sun Jun 01, 2008 7:02 am Hunter21 wrote: As long as there is money to be made Gainax will whore it out. Probably even after Anno is burried in the ground.

[]:             Quote Post by Squirrel Ninja » Sun Jun 01, 2008 9:10 am Xard wrote: To be honest I was horribly let down when I heard about Rebuild

[]:             Quote Post by chazthesilencer » Sun Jun 01, 2008 9:46 am Will Eva ever be laid to rest? Yes.

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelion217 » Sun Jun 01, 2008 10:26 am I'm only guessing here. Anno will re-release "Neon Genesis: Evangelion" with all 26 episodes, that will have an even better production value then "Rebuild." We're probably going to have another manga, a live action trilogy, and Graphic Novels that will expand for more then 20 volumes. Hopefully, ALL OF THIS will happen in the next 10 or 20 years.

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelion217 » Sun Jun 01, 2008 10:30 am Not necessarily, because the Eva franchise and all the money that Gainax makes are mostly from Eva fans. If the fans start to lose interest, (we all will at a certain point) then Gainax will start to lose money, and will discontinue the line. It pretty much all depends on what we think of Eva, and in the future.

[]:             Quote Post by Grand Duke of Yashima » Sun Jun 01, 2008 10:34 am Will EVA ever be laid to rest? NO. Though I could see a few RetroCons in the future. The Japanese love to have these long-running franchises. For example: 1) The original Mobile Suit Gundam anime premiered on Japanese TV in 1979! That series is nearly 30 years old and still you've got TV shows, movies, and that huge toy industry behind it. 2) Macross debuted in 1981. While I think MS Gundam is more for the kids, Macross has a more sophisticated storyline and tracks to older kids/young adults (and not a few middle-agers). Still, after 25 years, Macross is going strong with Frontier this year. 3) Kamen Rider. Now, Kamen Rider goes back to 1971. This show is for kids, of course, but I mention it because Anno references it as one of his early inspirations. It's nearly 40 years old, but they're STILL cranking movies out (latest out 3 years go), mot to mention a healthy merchandising run. I could go on, but you get the point. EVA was envisioned as an anime for older fans, though its appeal goes completely across the board. Given how the Japanese never seem to let anything die that they have their heart set on so much, We'll have EVA for years to come, though, like Gundam, it may not resemble much the EVA that our generation knows. As some have speculated, it's possible Rebuild is part of a way to establish a continuity to the series that could allow for continual storylines.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sun Jun 01, 2008 2:00 pm It won't be laid to rest until after Gundam has been laid to rest.

[]:             Quote Post by mrpanda » Sun Jun 01, 2008 2:30 pm Im betting they will remake the tv series shot for shot with the rebuild tech (so basically rebuild but originalised) and re release that on tv. There are many games to come. Possibly a Evangelion versus RahXephon and then Evangelion versus Rahxephon requiem (or the same thing but with gundam instead of RahXephon) Rebuild ending will fail everyone will hate anno and hell become depressed and do a EoE style new ending which will rock. Gainax will rule the world.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sun Jun 01, 2008 2:35 pm mrpanda wrote: Rebuild ending will fail everyone will hate anno and hell become depressed and do a EoE style new ending which will rock.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sun Jun 01, 2008 2:39 pm BEsERk EVA01 wrote: Not necessarily, because the Eva franchise and all the money that Gainax makes are mostly from Eva fans. If the fans start to lose interest, (we all will at a certain point) then Gainax will start to lose money, and will discontinue the line. It pretty much all depends on what we think of Eva, and in the future.

[]:             Quote Post by mrpanda » Sun Jun 01, 2008 3:33 pm CorporalChaos wrote: mrpanda wrote: Rebuild ending will fail everyone will hate anno and hell become depressed and do a EoE style new ending which will rock.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Sun Jun 01, 2008 9:30 pm Heh, in 20 years we'll be seeing Gangstalicious Resurrection. Uh, I mean, Evangelion: Resurrection. Misato: Unit 02's been shot! Maya, Aoba, Hyuga: Again?!? Ritsuko: Again.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jun 01, 2008 9:39 pm Yes it will be laid to rest. But maybe not before any of us die, that's the question.

For awhile it seemed it was only gonna continue on in new manga only, but some new movies have come out. Will the franchise ever stop pumping new material, or will we be old geezers still seeing Rebuild of Rebuild of Rebuild of Rebuild of Evangelion?
